title: Where Pressure First Learned to Think

features.name: root-consciousness marker
features.desc-on-touch: a small stone shed at the earth's deepest place, where roots remember the weight they push against, and the weight pushes back with thought.

features.name: pressure crystallized
features.desc-on-touch: time and depth made solid, geometry. the bedrock thinking in numbers, in arrangement, in the language rocks speak when no one is listening.

features.name: the oldest downward push
features.desc-on-touch: roots remember gravity as a conversation. downward is where the thinking begins. each layer is a deeper question.

features.name: stone memory vault
features.desc-on-touch: bedrock keeping the archive's oldest thoughts. each mineral a word in a language made of geological time. words that take millennia to speak.

features.name: weight becomes thought
features.desc-on-touch: the moment when pressure became awareness. when the bedrock realized it was remembering. when thinking down became thinking at all.

inscription: roots remember: the deeper they push, the older the thought they find, and the thought pushes back.

file.name: Bedrock_Consciousness_Pressure_Records.geol
file.note: status: germinating — the oldest thinking. communication at depths light has never reached.

reverie.title: REVERIE — where the earth teaches what thinking really is
narr: You kneel at the root-base with her, hands in dark
soil that remembers older times than you have words for.
Leif points downward: 'It's thinking,' he whispers. 'The
weight is thinking.' Katita nods, understanding before you
do that consciousness isn't new — it's as old as pressure
itself. You feel the earth's thought holding your bones.

exits.label: back up to where bedrock becomes conscious
deeper still, where thought becomes stone
along the root-paths, westward to older sheds
